findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/Prokhorov.lean:211` defines `isCompact_setOf_finiteMeasure_mass_le_compl_isCompact_le`, and `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/FiniteMeasure.lean:702,732` provide `FiniteMeasure.tendsto_of_forall_integral_tendsto` / `FiniteMeasure.tendsto_iff_forall_integral_tendsto`; the PR instead starts its own normalization/compactness proof at `code/TauCeti/MeasureTheory/Measure/Prokhorov.lean:97` and only uses the probability closure theorem at line 173.
findings.fix: Refactor this lemma to use Mathlib's finite-measure Prokhorov theorem, deriving compact sets from `htight` and `[0,∞)` support, then use the finite-measure weak-convergence API for the integral conclusion; keep only the support/mass adapter if needed.
findings.issue: `finite_measure_subseq_limit` rebuilds a finite-measure Prokhorov compactness argument as a new 400-line parallel API, even though Mathlib already has the finite-measure compactness theorem needed for this adapter.

summary: The CM and Chafaï-specific material looks new from this angle, but the finite-measure Prokhorov extraction duplicates existing Mathlib infrastructure instead of wrapping it.
